pi_items.verified_value: ct_cranium/0051 - Official InVesalius v3.0 sample dataset from https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip
pi_items.source: setup_invesalius.sh (lines 71-73) and official InVesalius download page (https://invesalius.github.io/download.html)

Web search shows bizygomatic breadth measurements: U.S. females 133.77 ± 5.56 mm, males 142.62 ± 6.22 mm; various population ranges 108-150 mm. Value of 110.0 is at lower end of human range but cannot be definitively verified.

Web search shows bizygomatic breadth measurements typically range up to 142-149 mm. Value of 160.0 is higher than most reported anthropometric data (see sources: https://medcraveonline.com/MOJAP/MOJAP-03-00081, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9554718/, https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/data_research/research/med_humanfacs/oamtechreports/AM93-10.pdf). Appears to be a conservative safety margin but not contradicted.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the official InVesalius v3.0 CT Cranium sample dataset (case 0051), a real medical imaging dataset publicly available from the InVesalius GitHub releases. The output path is verified as /home/ga/Documents/skull_3d_measure.inv3. The measurement range of 110-160 mm for bizygomatic width encompasses published anthropometric values from multiple populations, though the maximum of 160 mm exceeds most reported clinical measurements (typically 133-142 mm for adult females and males respectively).
